__fixture_tag__: VENDORED SOURCE SNAPSHOT — frozen citation, not live data

claim.property: reactive dye covalent yield on cellulose
claim.material: Procion / Drimarene / Remazol-class reactive dye on cotton cellulose
claim.condition: 60 C, pH 11 (Na2CO3 alkali fixation); Soxhlet wash-off + UV-Vis residual absorbance; n >= 3 swatches

source.primary: ISO 105-X12 — colorfastness to rubbing (crocking) test method for reactive-dyed fabrics
source.secondary: ICI Procion-H technical manual (industrial reactive-dye fixation envelope); Aspland J.R. 1997 'Textile Dyeing and Coloration', AATCC
source.citation: ISO 105-X12 + ICI Procion-H + Aspland 1997 — reactive dye covalent yield >= 60 % on cellulose at 60 C, pH 11

tolerance.basis: industrial reactive-dye envelope: covalent yield must be >= 60 % at 60 C / pH 11 to qualify as reactive-grade; < 50 % retires the claim

spec_doc.line_hint: §3.1 Reactive dye block: F-FAS-Q1 falsifier '>= 60 % on cellulose at 60 C, pH 11'
